description_long: This is a standard hand screwdriver featuring a contoured, ergonomic handle made of dark green plastic with subtle texturing for grip. The handle is bulbous in the middle and tapers toward both ends, providing a comfortable hold for manual use. The shaft is metallic, straight, and slender, extending from the handle and terminating in a flathead tip suitable for slotted screws. The handle appears solid and robust, with no visible cracks or damage, and the shaft is securely embedded into the handle. The overall design is utilitarian, typical for tools found in garages, workshops, or urban maintenance settings. No visible branding or markings are present on the handle or shaft.
description: A green, contoured plastic-handled screwdriver with a metallic flathead tip and a robust, ergonomic design. The tool is clean and unmarked, suitable for general manual tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the screwdriver, highlighting the contoured green handle and straight metallic shaft with a flathead tip.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, displaying its rounded, ergonomic shape and the alignment of the shaft emerging from the center.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the handle's contours and the straight shaft with the flathead tip.
description_view_3: End-on view of the tip, showing the flathead blade and the handle's rear profile.
attribute_handle_type: contoured, plastic, ergonomic, textured for grip
